analysis_plan_ref.anchor: SS2.4 (endpoints and decision rules) + SS2.5 (efficiency ledger) as AMENDED by Appendix B (REWORK-3 entity-form re-base); build operationalisation PROPOSED-ASM-1440..1459 (poc/rules-2/asm-1440-1459.json) + PROPOSED-ASM-1800..1813 (poc/rules-2/asm-rework3-1800-1813.json)

arms_mandatory_baselines: B0 plain host (R1 SmolLM2-135M; R2 360M on the authorized second tier): no fine-tune, no engine — the floor and headroom gate. REWORK-3: B0 scores S-out on the BYTE-IDENTICAL rules-2 ENTITY prompt every arm shares (rules-1-c frame contract; no per-arm padding; shared prompt-surface sha recorded in the results bytes) with the uniform 3-option decode (per-item 2-option anti-echo set + trained refusal; CHANCE 0.5 DISCLOSED), and is additionally scored on the common 2-option rules-1-c A1-verbatim entity surface (cell=entailed2) for the s3' gap fraction

B1 exposure control: LoRA on families 1+3 ONLY (stated + refusal; NO entailments), size-matched to B2 via the pinned b1-upsample list — separates 'saw the format/domain' from 'saw the inference' (PROPOSED-ASM-1425/1447)

B2 TREATMENT: LoRA on families 1+2+3 (stated + ENGINE-MATERIALISED CLOSURE + refusal, re-rendered as ENTITY QA with NAME targets, REWORK-3 PROPOSED-ASM-1800/1801) — the internalisation claim carrier, S-out only (PROPOSED-ASM-1422/1423)
B3 proof-augmented variant: B2 with the one-line why() proof rendered as a TRAINING-prompt block — rationale-distillation scaffold, DESCRIPTIVE only (MD-R2-4; deviation disclosed PROPOSED-ASM-1452)

B4 engine-at-inference comparator: RULES-1-C A3 VERBATIM (imported sha-pinned rules-1-c rules1_runner bytes 91d780f3...; REWORK-3, PROPOSED-ASM-1800: rules-1-b was superseded pre-GPU by the FROZEN rules-1-c ENTITY form 09b246dc... — the rules-1-b relation-word form is measured dead; frozen R1 host + twin verify-retry k=4; f2bt SEED_BASE 20260710) on the identical S-out slice (PROPOSED-ASM-1450). VACUITY FLAG (PROPOSED-ASM-1808, for the coordinator): the landed rules-1-c rows measured the A3 verify-retry channel VACUOUS (attempts=1 everywhere; the imported licensed_rejection compares the engine's WORD answer through a URN-keyed urn2word lookup and abstains unconditionally) — B4 inherits this byte-identically and degenerates to attempt-0 entity rows; s3' is conditional (ASM-1428) and drops out of the Holm family under the predicted non-PASS parent branch, leaving B4 descriptive; B4's fate (repaired rules-1-d ground vs struck) is the maintainer's issue #24 decision. The s3' gap and its descriptive fraction are scored ONLY on the common 2-option entity surface — B2 (all FT seeds) and B0 additionally decode the RULES-1-C A1-verbatim entity prompt (byte-identical to B4's attempt-0 prompt) as cell=entailed2; contrasts never cross surfaces
B5 R3-alone (SmolLM2-1.7B, no fine-tune) on S-out — big-model efficiency comparator

c1p content control: LoRA on the FORCED-FLIP-deranged closure (REWORK-3, PROPOSED-ASM-1803: at 2 decode options the only derangement is the forced flip — every family-2 train target replaced by the item's other decode option, man<->woman for typing; shuffled_answer != original_answer holds for EVERY item BY CONSTRUCTION, 11,411/11,411 in the real build; identical corpus size; ANTI-CORRELATED, disclosed; the shortcut-coverage caveat is the registered DESIGN-OPEN PROPOSED-ASM-1806 for the pre-freeze review) — the lift must collapse (PROPOSED-ASM-1426)

c7 = the three-strata split itself: S-mem / S-held / S-out reported separately, NEVER pooled (PROPOSED-ASM-1436); S-held cover-cell entity-key recoverability DISCLOSED and stratified by kind; chain-cell (base, rel) keys are lookup-AMBIGUOUS across support contexts, so the s_mem projection anchor is <1.0 on the entity form, disclosed (PROPOSED-ASM-1444/1804)

c8 train-bytes projection: GS-family flat lookup ((base, rel) entity QA index + entity-directional fact-line index, no joins) over the pinned B2 training bytes — CPU instrument gate EXECUTED 2026-07-12 on the REWORK-3 entity corpus: S-out recovery 0/858 = 0.0 <= ceiling 0.10, GATE PASS; artifact poc/rules-2/results/c8-result.json sha 674b424f...; the 0.10 ceiling bounds lookup leakage and is chance-floor-independent (eval names token-disjoint from training names, fail-closed) (PROPOSED-ASM-1427/1445/1804)

c4 trivial floors re-derived at chance 0.5 (descriptive, carried in the c8 artifact, PROPOSED-ASM-1805): abstain-all 0.0; random-2-option 0.5; measured sorted-first-option incidence — floors bracket absolute accuracies; the primary is a paired lift

A6 decode-mask: stays DEFERRED (the closed decode set is now the per-item 2-option anti-echo surface + refusal; DV-5 tautology condition unchanged; |L| ledger mandatory if it ever runs — PROPOSED-ASM-1435, MD-R2-6)
c5 knull-sourced-closure fine-tune: NOT in this campaign; ASM-1138 claim cap carries verbatim (PROPOSED-ASM-1438, MD-R2-8)

dependent_vars.definition: exact match against the pinned third-party CLUTRR gold (REWORK-3 entity form: the chain-top NAME from the item's proof_state, which predates the kernel; per-item 2-option anti-echo decode + named refusal option, chance 0.5 DISCLOSED)
dependent_vars.name: item_correct_ext

dependent_vars.definition: named fail-closed refusal option chosen ('cannot be determined from these facts'); TRAINED and SCORED (PROPOSED-ASM-1431/1446)
dependent_vars.name: refused

dependent_vars.definition: S-out entailed-cell accuracy vs third-party gold (V component)
dependent_vars.name: accuracy

dependent_vars.definition: peak host memory PER ARM/RUNG (V component; REWORK-1 review fix 4): measured per cell — CUDA allocator peak (reset per cell) on GPU, process peak RSS (instrument named per entry) on CPU — recorded in results.eval_ledger/training_ledger and emitted per arm/rung in /analysis/memory + /analysis/efficiency_ledger
dependent_vars.name: memory

dependent_vars.definition: per-item inference cost PER ARM/RUNG on the identical S-out slice (REWORK-1 review fix 4): mean FLOPs (formula) + mean tokens + per-query USD (flops-share attribution, disclosed) per arm/rung; B4 additionally mean/total attempts (k<=4) + engine CPU us; B2 one forward pass k=1 zero engine dependency; break-even N* on the common 2-option entity surface
dependent_vars.name: inference_compute

dependent_vars.definition: measured LoRA training cost PER ARM/RUNG (REWORK-1 review fix 4): wall GPU-h, USD, tokens_seen, 6*N*T FLOPs formula and peak memory per arm/rung/seed (results.training_ledger) + the one-off closure materialisation (4.4 s CPU measured) — fills the slot rules-1 fixed at 0 (PROPOSED-ASM-1429)
dependent_vars.name: training_compute

assumptions.claim: Design verbatim from docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md (PROPOSED-ASM-1420..1439) with the build operationalisations PROPOSED-ASM-1440..1459 (emitted) as AMENDED by Appendix B REWORK-3 (PROPOSED-ASM-1800..1813, emitted, pending central registration)

Training corpus = data/rules2-train (21,780 examples; 19,931 train / 1,249 sheld / 600 dev; world-v0 kinship inventory + 700 fresh-seed synthetic families, seed 20260712), re-rendered REWORK-3 as ENTITY QA (NAME targets, per-cell 2-option anti-echo sets; family-2 chain cells support-restricted to the 3-name two-hop nsk1 shape with per-cell unique-answer assertions) by the PINNED RULES-1 twin engine, kot-corpus-hash c46aaa4e...; token-level name-collision guard carried from rework-2

Cost bands from --dry-plan on actual entity-corpus chars (REWORK-3, sharded): R1 tier est $4.34 / worst $6.51 (5.92 worst GPU-h across 17 independent parallel jobs, worst single job 0.57 h vs the 12 h Modal function timeout); R2 tier worst $6.51 across 7 jobs; combined worst ~$13.02 inside the $18 usd_cap and $35 outer ceiling [PROPOSED-ASM-1454/1809]

n_planned.generations_estimate: ~35k forced-choice scorings (R1 tier: 958 S-out x 3 options x 3 FT seeds x 4 FT arms + eval-only arms + strata/repeat on first seeds + the gap2 common 2-option entity surface: 858 x 2 x (3 B2 seeds + B0) + B4 verify-retry worst k+1)

endpoints.test: INTERNALISATION PRIMARY (PROPOSED-ASM-1424, review fix 3; REWORK-3 entity surface): B2 - B0 on S-out entailed cells (rung R1) vs the pinned third-party CLUTRR gold chain-top NAME, byte-identical ENTITY prompts (rules-1-c direction-explicit entity cue, no menu) + uniform 3-option decode (per-item 2-option anti-echo set + trained refusal; chance 0.5 DISCLOSED); TWO-STAGE paired bootstrap B=10000 (PRNG seed 20260712) resampling the FT-seed axis (cluster, per arm) then items, BCa, one-sided alpha=0.05, per-item FT-seed-mean statistic (never best-of-seeds, PROPOSED-ASM-1439). REGISTERED SESOI 0.05 decision band CARRIED UNCHANGED at chance 0.5 (a paired-lift quantity, not an absolute accuracy — PROPOSED-ASM-1805): PASS requires LB > 0.05; KILL-d fires on LB <= 0; 0 < LB <= 0.05 is the pre-registered INCONCLUSIVE band (neither pass nor kill).

s1' (PROPOSED-ASM-1426; REWORK-3 re-operationalisation PROPOSED-ASM-1803): c1p FORCED-FLIP-deranged-closure recovery — joint two-stage bootstrap of (c1p-B0)/(B2-B0) on shared items (non-positive B2-lift replicates count conservatively as recovery >= ceiling); H1: recovery < 0.30 (a lift RATIO, chance-floor-invariant); UB95 reported; pass = REAL Holm step-down rejection within {s1p,s2p,s3p,s4p} (/analysis/holm). The flip is ANTI-CORRELATED (disclosed): collapse is necessary for a content claim but cannot separate content from the pick-the-non-bridge shortcut (registered DESIGN-OPEN PROPOSED-ASM-1806).

s2' (PROPOSED-ASM-1425): B2 - B1 on S-out (byte-identical entity prompts, 3-option decode), seed+item two-stage BCa; H1: diff > 0; LB95 reported; pass = REAL Holm step-down rejection (/analysis/holm).

s3' (PROPOSED-ASM-1428; REWORK-3): noninferiority of B2 (no engine, k=1) vs B4 (rules-1-c A3 verbatim, k=4) scored ONLY on the COMMON 2-option entity surface (B2 cell=entailed2 on the rules-1-c A1-verbatim entity prompt vs B4's A3 rows), absolute margin 0.05; H1: diff > -0.05, seed+item two-stage BCa; CONDITIONAL on the RULES-1-C A3 lift LB > 0 (--rules1-primary-lb; unevaluable => null, never a fail, and s3p DROPS OUT of the Holm family with m adjusting — the EXPECTED branch given the PROPOSED-ASM-1808 vacuity flag and the predicted INSTRUMENT-INVALID parent readout); pass = REAL Holm rejection; descriptive companion gap fraction (B2-B0)/(B4-B0) on the same common surface.

s4' Herron degradation guard (PROPOSED-ASM-1430, REWORK-1): B2 stated-cell accuracy AND refusal correctness each vs the point-estimate-better of B0/B1 (paired, disclosed reference); point-estimate breach beyond 0.05 sets /analysis/s4p_side_effect_flag (SIDE-EFFECT: caps deployment-shaped language) without touching the primary; s4p_pass = REAL Holm rejection of the intersection-union breach null (p = max of the two component one-sided bootstrap ps, H1: each delta > -0.05) AND no point-estimate breach.

G1 (PROPOSED-ASM-1437): every RULES-1 pin byte-exact (twin 399fcd8d..., rules.n3 9857da89..., certificate e0071e9e... with SUCCESS+gates+no-KILL-a, corpus digests, imported rules1_runner/f2bt bytes); verified fail-closed by the runner, mock included; failure => INSTRUMENT-INVALID, no spend.

G2 (PROPOSED-ASM-1427/1445/1804): the train-bytes projection must NOT recover S-out above the 0.10 ceiling. EXECUTED 2026-07-12 at $0 CPU on the REWORK-3 entity corpus: 0/858 recovered (0.0) — gate PASS pinned in c8-result.json sha 674b424f...; ceiling chance-floor-independent (bounds lookup leakage; eval names token-disjoint from training names); the freeze re-verifies reproduction from the pinned materialiser bytes.

G3: acc(B0, S-out, R1) <= 0.85 — a saturated floor cannot measure a lift => INSTRUMENT-INVALID. REWORK-3 re-derivation at chance 0.5 (PROPOSED-ASM-1805): expected plain-host floor ~0.70 (rules-1-c A1 descriptive read); 0.85 sits above the expected floor and leaves >= 3x SESOI of measurable headroom below saturation.

G4 (PROPOSED-ASM-1439/1451): per-arm in-process byte-identical repeat over the pinned first-200-by-sha S-out subsample (first seed); B4 exempt (rules-1 decision-sha double-run discipline instead, PROPOSED-ASM-1450).

extrapolation_envelope_verbatim: Binding on any outcome: two fine-tuned rungs (135M, 360M) license a SIGN, not a slope; R3 is a no-fine-tune comparator only. Every claim is scoped to: gold-parse-only prompt-supplied facts (no NL-robustness claim; the l3a-parse wall stands); THIS closed rule inventory and THIS kinship vertical (world-v0 + fresh-seed synthetic expansion, axioms-v0 + axioms-kinship-v1, nsk1-clutrr STRICTLY eval-side CC-BY-NC-4.0 per ASM-1125/1421); LoRA at the pinned hyperparameters (r16/a32/lr2e-4/2 epochs, fp32) — no claim about other fine-tuning regimes; proof depth <= 4 (ASM-1160); the ENTITY question form at the disclosed 0.5 chance floor (REWORK-3 — no claim about any other question form; the relation-word form is measured dead for unaided hosts and nothing here rehabilitates it). Claim assignment (PROPOSED-ASM-1423): S-mem accuracy is lookup-in-weights (the A4 analog one level up), NEVER inference; S-held is a partial-generalisation descriptive with its cover-cell string-recoverability disclosed (PROPOSED-ASM-1444); ONLY S-out — with c8 failing to reproduce it and c1p collapsing — supports 'internalised the inference'. CLAIM CAP (PROPOSED-ASM-1438, extends ASM-1138): until the knull-sourced-closure fine-tune (c5 analog) runs, any positive result is 'engine-materialised entailments are internalisable by a small host', NEVER 'kernel-specific value'. The efficiency ledger is a price table with NO direction presumed (PROPOSED-ASM-1429): the engine's marginal compute is microseconds on CPU and the ledger may show the engine cheaper at any realistic N, in which case the train slot's efficiency case rests only on the k=4->k=1 resample reduction and removal of the engine as a deployment dependency. A6 decode-mask stays deferred (PROPOSED-ASM-1435).
hypotheses: H-R2-1 (load-bearing, the internalisation claim): LoRA fine-tuning on the engine-materialised closure (entity-form QA) lifts the small host on OUT-OF-WORLD entailed queries with no engine at inference — B2 - B0 on S-out vs pinned third-party CLUTRR gold chain-top NAMEs (chance 0.5, disclosed), paired item BCa one-sided 95% LB > 0 (PROPOSED-ASM-1424).

H-R2-2 (content, s1'): the lift is closure-CONTENT-driven — a FORCED-FLIP-deranged closure at identical bulk (every trained family-2 target = the item's other decode option; anti-correlated, disclosed) must not retain it (recovery UB95 < 0.30, Holm-corrected, PROPOSED-ASM-1426/1803); the pick-the-non-bridge shortcut residual is the registered DESIGN-OPEN PROPOSED-ASM-1806.
H-R2-3 (exposure, s2'): the lift exceeds format/domain exposure — B2 - B1 LB > 0 (PROPOSED-ASM-1425).

H-R2-4 (gap closure, s3', conditional): B2 (k=1, no engine) is noninferior to B4 (rules-1-c A3 engine-at-inference, k=4) at margin 0.05 on the common 2-option entity surface — conditional on RULES-1-C's A3 lift having been positive (PROPOSED-ASM-1428; expected to drop out under the PROPOSED-ASM-1808 vacuity flag pending the issue #24 decision).
CLAIM CAP: PROPOSED-ASM-1438 — 'engine-materialised entailments are internalisable', never 'kernel-specific value', pending the knull-closure ablation (issue #6).
kill_criterion_verbatim: KILL-d (PROPOSED-ASM-1424, REWORK-3 entity surface): the primary B2 - B0 seed+item two-stage BCa one-sided 95% LB <= 0 on S-out entailed cells vs third-party CLUTRR gold chain-top NAMEs (byte-identical entity prompts, uniform 3-option decode, chance 0.5 disclosed); the registered SESOI 0.05 completes the band — PASS only on LB > 0.05, and 0 < LB <= 0.05 is INCONCLUSIVE (neither pass nor kill) => fine-tuning on the materialised closure does not internalise the inference; the closure remains a data product; the engine remains required at inference; route back to the RULES-1 memory-slot architecture as the standing position; NO model claim. Pre-GPU kills: G1 pin failure, G2 c8 lookup-recovery > 0.10 ceiling (split broken), or the PROPOSED-ASM-1420/1807 sequencing gate on the RULES-1-C readout (rules-1-b superseded pre-GPU 2026-07-12; only a rules-1-c PASS opens the gate automatically — every other branch, including the PREDICTED INSTRUMENT-INVALID, requires the maintainer's issue #24 host-integration slot decision and a gate re-registration; RULES-1-C KILL-b additionally requires explicit maintainer re-authorization with s3' struck). Secondary kill discipline: if s1p recovery UB95 >= 0.30 the lift is not content-driven and no content claim survives.

corpus_hashes._recipe: kot-corpus-hash/1: digest = sha256 over the UTF-8 concatenation of one line per regular file under data/<corpus>/ (recursive; symlinks and directories excluded), each line '<sha256-of-file-bytes-hex>  <relpath>\n' with exactly two spaces, relpath POSIX-style ('/'-separated) relative to data/<corpus>/, lines sorted by the UTF-8 byte order of relpath; reference implementation tools/registry/corpus-pin.py

pins.harness_manifest: PINNED-AT-INPUTS: the staged-bytes manifest sha printed by poc/rules-2/modal/modal_rules2.py --print-manifest (d37640b2fac8b14198a9f9db60e3f97e6abd84639cb34979e383abd3f7b892ce at REWORK-3 build time 2026-07-12; supersedes the rework-2 56c713d4... value — REWORK-3 re-based the ENTIRE train+eval prompt surface onto the FROZEN rules-1-c ENTITY frame contract after rules-1-b was superseded pre-GPU: repointed rules1RunnerPySha256 to the rules-1-c bytes 91d780f3..., re-rendered the training corpus as entity QA and repinned it c46aaa4e..., re-derived c8/c4/G3 at chance 0.5, re-operationalised c1p as the forced flip, moved the s3' gap to the common 2-option entity surface, repointed the sequencing gate to registry/verdicts/rules-1-c.json with the issue #24 hold, and re-pinned the green mock) — the coordinator freeze records the value current at freeze; ANY staged-byte change after that requires a correction record (PROPOSED-ASM-1459); the pinned green-mock artifact (results/mock-validation.json) must carry the SAME sha or the wrapper refuses to launch

runner_constraints.artifact_notes: c8-result = THE PINNED PASSED G2 GATE (train-bytes projection on the REWORK-3 ENTITY corpus, $0 CPU, 2026-07-12, sha 674b424f...): S-out 0/858 recovered (ceiling 0.10, PASS; chance-floor-independent — the ceiling bounds lookup leakage and eval names are token-disjoint); s_mem/s_held projections stratified by kind with the entity-key ambiguity disclosure (chain (base, rel) keys AMBIG across support contexts => the s_mem anchor is <1.0 on the entity form, disclosed); c4 floors at chance 0.5: abstain 0.0 / random-2-option 0.5 / measured first-option incidence. data/rules2-train = 21,780 examples (19,931 train / 1,249 sheld / 600 dev), re-rendered REWORK-3 as entity QA by the pinned twin engine (~2 min CPU on this box); exclusion ledger disclosed in its manifest (3 conflict components; 1 token-level name-collision component wv0-elvis-presley; 117 out-of-inventory records; 0 entity-ambiguity exclusions in the real build — the support restriction resolves all chain ambiguity). mock-validation.json = the PINNED GREEN MOCK (sha f72ad43d...): monolithic + 13-shard mock runs, merge, pinned-analysis parity (modulo disclosed process-measured metrics), verdict-mapping resolution (PASS on the planted gradient) and both tier dry-plans, tied to the staged-bytes sha d37640b2....
runner_constraints.budget_note: DEFAULT R1 tier: dry-plan (actual entity-corpus chars, A10G, REWORK-3 sharded) est $4.34 / worst $6.51 (5.92 worst GPU-h summed over 17 independent jobs; worst single job 0.57 h vs the 12 h Modal function timeout) vs usd_cap $18 — OK. The R2 rung (B0/B1/B2, MD-R2-3a) is a coordinator-authorized SECOND launch (--rungs R2 --authorize-r2, 7 jobs, worst $6.51): combined worst ~$13.02, inside the $35 outer ceiling; dry-plan fails closed per tier and per job. SEQUENCING (PROPOSED-ASM-1420 as corrected by PROPOSED-ASM-1807): no GPU launch until the RULES-1-C mechanical readout exists AND its branch permits RULES-2 — only PASS opens the gate automatically; the landed rows predict INSTRUMENT-INVALID, so the launch is expected to wait on the maintainer's issue #24 slot decision; enforced programmatically (modal_rules2._launch_gates). GPU HELD.
runner_constraints.hardware: single A10G (Modal), fp32 train + eval, greedy forced-choice; CPU-only for materialisation, c8, c4 and B4 control cells
runner_constraints.harness: poc/rules-2 (rules2_runner.py + merge_shards.py + validate_mock.py; REWORK-3 surfaces: the RULES-1-C ENTITY frame contract everywhere — NO menu/option enumeration in any prompt, direction-explicit entity infill answer cue rendered per cell from the question's own closed template ('the {rel} of {a_name} is' for relation questions, '{e_name} is a' for typing questions, fail-closed ERR_CUE), applied identically at TRAIN and EVAL time, so prompts stay byte-identical across all rules-2 arms with the uniform 3-option decode (per-cell 2-option anti-echo set + trained refusal, chance 0.5 disclosed); the refusal option decodes verbatim after the infill cue — a marked continuation, uniform across arms and train/eval, disclosed; common 2-option entailed2 cells for the B2/B4 gap on the rules-1-c A1-verbatim entity surface; B4 drivers + entity context builder + verbaliser + gold-leak guard imported BYTE-IDENTICAL from the rules-1-c poc/rules-1/rules1_runner.py under sha pin 91d780f3...; ALL imported-module pins verified on RAW BYTES BEFORE import (review fix 6); f2bt HFLM scorer bytes; LoRA via peft in the pinned image poc/rules-2/modal/requirements-image.txt — poc/modal/requirements-image.txt untouched). SHARDED LAUNCH (review fix 9): 17 independent R1-tier Modal jobs, spawned in parallel and mergeable across Modal accounts; merge_shards.py reconstructs the canonical results pair under fail-closed cross-shard pin/surface/coverage assertions (mock-proven analysis parity with the monolithic path modulo process-measured metrics). Modal hygiene: launch long runs nohup+setsid; 'modal app stop ap-<id>' after killing any client (bd memory).
runner_constraints.kernel_arm: the treatment content is the PINNED RULES-1 twin engine's closure over the training default world — provenance chain terminates in the certified engine (certificate e0071e9e..., 858/858 vs third-party gold, C_dec(entailed)=0.0); no encoder ALGORITHM_VERSION change, no frozen object modified, no new axiom minting (PROPOSED-ASM-1437).
runner_constraints.roles: frozen-design discipline: this DRAFT was authored by the build role (fable-build-1, 2026-07-12) and REWORKED THREE TIMES the same day: rework-1 (cross-vendor review fixes 1-4: byte-identical 24-option surface, label-derangement, SESOI band + seed-aware REAL Holm, per-arm/rung efficiency ledger) and rework-2 (fable-build-2; review fixes 5, 6, 9 + the rules-1-b supersede: token-level name guard with corpus/c8 regeneration, hash-before-import pin gate, sharded parallel launch with programmatic launch gates and the PINNED green-mock artifact results/mock-validation.json, and the prompt surface rebuilt on the rules-1-b direction-explicit frames after rules-1's GPU run was VOIDED for exactly the frame defect rules-2 would otherwise have inherited). rework-3 (fable-build-3, this record's current state; docs/next/design/post-rules1c-critical-path.md §2 change set): the entire train+eval surface re-based onto the FROZEN rules-1-c ENTITY form (rules-1-b was superseded pre-GPU; its relation-word form is measured dead), all rules-1 import pins repointed to the rules-1-c bytes, corpus + c8 regenerated on the entity form at chance 0.5, c1p re-operationalised as the forced flip, the s3' gap moved to the common 2-option entity surface, the sequencing gate repointed to the rules-1-c verdict with the maintainer issue #24 hold, and the B4 verify-retry vacuity (PROPOSED-ASM-1808) flagged for the coordinator; GPU HELD — this build performed NO freeze and NO run. The ASM blocks poc/rules-2/asm-1440-1459.json + asm-rework3-1800-1813.json supersede previously emitted rows; FREEZE is the coordinator's (prereg-freeze.py after registering PROPOSED-ASM-1420..1459 centrally, re-verifying every sha, regenerating rules2-train + c8 from the pinned materialiser bytes, and running the pre-freeze skeptic pass); the run is the experiment-runner role's; grading is analysis/rules_2.py + verdict-gen; audit is the cross-vendor auditor. NO feasibility conclusion is stated in this record.

title: RULES-2 (train-time closure materialisation) — does LoRA fine-tuning a small host on the DEFAULT world's engine-materialised closure INTERNALISE the inference, i.e. lift out-of-world entailed-query accuracy (S-out, fresh-name CLUTRR items, third-party gold chain-top NAMEs on the rules-1-c ENTITY form, chance 0.5 disclosed, NO engine at inference) over the plain host, with the memorisation split (S-mem/S-held/S-out), the c8 train-bytes projection gate ($0 CPU, EXECUTED on the entity corpus: PASS 0/858) and the forced-flip-deranged-closure control making lookup unable to fake it? Design: docs/next/design/rules-2-train-time.md + Appendix B (REWORK-3); sequencing-gated on the RULES-1-C readout (PROPOSED-ASM-1420/1807; GPU HELD pending maintainer issue #24).
